author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! āra ki emana daśā haba
e⋅bhava⋅saṁsāra tyaji’, parama⋅ānande maji’,
āra kabe vraja-bhūme jā’ba

sukha-maya vṛndāvana, kabe ha’be daraśana,
se⋅dhūli lāgibe ka’be gāya
preme gadagada haiñā, rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅nāma laiyā,
kāndiyā beḍāba ubharāya

nibhṛte nikuñje jāñā, aṣṭāṅge praṇāma haiyā,
ḍākiba ‘hā rādhānātha’ bali’
kabe jamunāra tīre, paraśa kariba nīre,
kabe piba karapuṭe tuli’

āra kabe emana haba, śrī-rāsa-maṇḍale jā’ba,
kabe gaḍāgaḍi diba tāya
vaṁśīvaṭa⋅chāyā pāñā, parama⋅ānanda hañā,
paḍiyā rahiba tā’ra chāya

kabe govardhana giri, dekhiba nayana bhari’,
kabe habe rādhā⋅kuṇḍe vāsa
bhramite bhramite kabe, e⋅deha patana habe,
kahe dīna narottama⋅dāsa
en_translation:
O Lord Hari! Will such a state ever be mine? Abandoning this material existence and immersed in supreme bliss, when will I go to Vrajabhūmi?

When will I behold blissful Vṛndāvana? When will its dust touch my body? Overwhelmed with divine love and chanting the names of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, I will wander about weeping loudly.

Going to a secluded grove and offering my full obeisances, I will call out, "O Lord of Rādhā!" When will I touch the water on the bank of the Yamunā? When will I drink it from my cupped hands?

When will such a state be mine again? I will go to the place of the rāsa dance. When will I roll on the ground there? Finding the shade of the Vaṁśīvaṭa tree and becoming supremely joyful, I will remain there.

When will I see Govardhana Hill with my eyes full? When will I reside at Rādhā-kuṇḍa? While wandering around, when will this body fall? Thus says the humble Narottama dāsa.
